essence: Protects public health by inspecting food businesses, enforcing hygiene standards, and preventing food‑borne risks.

identity.title: Food Safety Officer
identity.summary: Inspects restaurants, takeaways, manufacturers, and retailers to ensure food is safe, hygienic, and compliant with regulations.

salary.notes: Senior officers or specialists in complex manufacturing environments can exceed £60k-£75k+.

qualifications: Training in food safety or environmental health
Knowledge of food‑hygiene legislation and enforcement
Ability to conduct inspections and sampling
Understanding of HACCP, contamination risks, and food‑borne illness prevention

lifestyle.description: Food safety officers work for councils and regulatory bodies, inspecting food businesses, investigating complaints, and enforcing hygiene standards. The role suits individuals who enjoy investigation, regulation, and protecting public health.

habits: Investigates food complaints and contamination risks
Writes clear inspection reports and notices
Communicates with business owners and the public
